title: Guernsey Has a Tortoise Escape Epidemic Right Now
script: Four tortoises. One morning? The Guernsey SPCA is overwhelmed. Their island is having a tortoise escape epidemic — and it's the heat's fault. Every summer, the shelter handles 20 to 30 lost tortoises. This week, 4 landed at the facility in a single 24-hour stretch. SPCA manager Steve Byrne explained it: tortoises are surprisingly good climbers, and when temperatures climb, they get motivated. The males especially wander in search of mates. Which means Guernsey just hit peak tortoise mating season during a full heat wave. Byrne says more escapes are coming as long as the temperatures hold. If you own a tortoise on this island, check your enclosure walls. Actually, check whether your tortoise is even home right now. Is anyone on Guernsey actually surprised that their tortoise isn't where they left it?
